title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens finished his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Boston Bruins GM Don Sweeney is considering signing Hagens to his entry-level contract following BC's season end.
Hagens, the 2025 NHL Draft's seventh pick, may boost the Bruins' lineup in the upcoming regular-season games.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man,' starring Cillian Murphy, streams on Netflix, set in 1940 Birmingham during WWII.
Bradley Cooper directs 'Is This Thing On?' featuring Will Arnett as a stand-up comic, streaming on Disney+.
'Born to Bowl,' a professional bowling docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber, is available on HBO Max.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora remains uncertain about his future with the Red Sox beyond his current contract ending in 2027.
Cora, now in his eighth season, is close to surpassing Terry Francona's win total but prioritizes playoff participation.
His future decisions will consider his family's needs, including schooling for his elementary-school-aged twins.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line, featuring Viktor Arvidsson, Pavel Zacha, and Casey Mittelstadt, has a 35-16 goal differential this season.
Boston's second line's goal differential of +19 is second only to the Colorado Avalanche's top line in the NHL.
Pavel Zacha leads the line with a career-high 23 goals and 51 points in 66 games during the 2025-26 season.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support MGH Pediatric Cancer Center.
Mahr was inspired to participate after witnessing young patients at Heartbreak Hill during his 2014 Boston Marathon run.
Pediatric cancer research receives limited funding compared to adult cancer, motivating Mahr to raise awareness and support.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A proposed Massachusetts income tax cut from 5% to 4% could reduce state revenue by $5.1 billion annually.
Boston.com poll shows 78% of respondents support the tax cut, while 20% oppose, and a few are unsure.
Governor Healey opposes the tax cut, arguing it endangers state finances, while supporters criticize current spending practices.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church is on trial for allegedly poisoning her boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze over a love triangle.
Prosecutors allege Church joked about poisoning Fowler and filmed him suffering from ethylene glycol poisoning.
Defense argues Church did not murder Fowler, citing her background and lack of direct evidence linking her to poisoning.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey proposes lifting Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban, expanding crossbow use, and reducing setback distances for bowhunters.
Massachusetts and Maine are the last U.S. states with a complete Sunday hunting ban, a remnant of historical 'blue laws'.
Overabundant deer populations exceeding management goals in Massachusetts drive support for changes to hunting laws and regulations.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cape Cod magnet fisher Nate DeMontigny found a derringer pistol in a case from a Bridgewater river.
The YouTube video of the discovery by Nate DeMontigny has garnered over 1,700 views since March 12.
DeMontigny contacted police immediately after finding the pistol, which appeared in pristine condition.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old faces murder charges after a Chelsea stabbing left one dead and others injured.
The incident occurred Thursday night near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue, according to Chelsea police.
Sergio Josue Castellanos will be arraigned in Chelsea District Court after a 25-year-old man died post-surgery.
